title: Severe-weather shelter decision log
summary: Record warning source, expected timing, wind or water direction, safest room, evacuation route, vulnerable people, animals, and the next decision time.
content: Record warning source, expected timing, wind or water direction, safest room, evacuation route, vulnerable people, animals, and the next decision time. Move before roads, stairs, bridges, or visibility become unsafe. Do not travel merely because the current location is uncomfortable when the route is more dangerous.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Severe-weather shelter decision log”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Severe-weather shelter decision log»
steps: Record warning source, expected timing, wind or water direction, safest room, evacuation route, vulnerable people, animals, and the next decision time.
Move before roads, stairs, bridges, or visibility become unsafe.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
warnings: Do not travel merely because the current location is uncomfortable when the route is more dangerous.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Severe-weather shelter decision log.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Severe-weather shelter decision log.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Severe-weather shelter decision log.
short_term: For Severe-weather shelter decision log, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Severe-weather shelter decision log, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Severe-weather shelter decision log, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Severe-weather shelter decision log to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
CDC — Keep food safe after an emergency: https://www.cdc.gov/food-safety/foods/keep-food-safe-after-emergency.html
American Red Cross — First aid steps: https://www.redcross.org/take-a-class/first-aid/performing-first-aid/first-aid-steps
